name: Todo App
description: A simple, intuitive, and feature-rich todo list web application designed specifically for students to manage their academic and personal tasks efficiently. The application aims to provide a seamless user experience, allowing students to prioritize tasks, set reminders, and track progress, ultimately enhancing their productivity and time management skills.
targetAudience: Students from high school to university levels, with a focus on those who require a structured and organized approach to managing their assignments, projects, and daily tasks.

strategy.vision: To become the leading todo list application for students worldwide, recognized for its simplicity, effectiveness, and innovative features that cater to the unique needs of the academic community, within the next five years.
strategy.mission: To design and develop a user-centric, accessible, and collaborative todo list web application that empowers students to achieve their academic and personal goals through better task management and time organization.
strategy.uniqueValueProp: A tailored todo list experience that integrates with popular educational platforms, offers AI-driven task suggestions, and provides personalized productivity insights, making it an indispensable tool for students seeking to enhance their academic performance and overall well-being.

strategicPillars.title: Student-Centric Design
strategicPillars.explanation: Developing the application with a deep understanding of students' needs, preferences, and behaviors, ensuring that every feature and interface element is designed to maximize user engagement and productivity.

strategicPillars.title: Integrative Technology
strategicPillars.explanation: Leveraging cutting-edge technologies and integrating with existing educational platforms to provide a seamless and connected experience, enhancing the application's utility and appeal to its target audience.

strategicPillars.title: AI-Driven Insights
strategicPillars.explanation: Utilizing artificial intelligence to analyze user behavior, provide personalized task recommendations, and offer insights into productivity patterns, helping students optimize their time management and study habits.

strategicPillars.title: Community Engagement
strategicPillars.explanation: Fostering a community around the application through features like shared task lists, study groups, and forums, where students can collaborate, share tips, and support one another in achieving their academic goals.

positioningMatrix.notes: The Todo App wins against incumbent solutions by offering a high degree of customizability without requiring extensive technical knowledge, coupled with seamless collaboration features that enable real-time interaction among users, all while maintaining a user-friendly interface and low friction API interactions.

roles.title: Lead Frontend Developer
responsibilities: Designing and implementing the user interface and user experience of the web application
Ensuring cross-browser compatibility and responsive design
roles.vibe: Focus on creating an intuitive and engaging user interface that simplifies task management for students.

roles.title: Backend Developer
responsibilities: Developing the server-side logic, database integration, and API connectivity
roles.vibe: Emphasis on building a robust, scalable, and secure backend infrastructure to support the application's features and user base.

roles.title: AI/ML Engineer
responsibilities: Designing and implementing AI-driven features such as task suggestions and productivity insights
Integrating machine learning models with the application's backend
roles.vibe: Innovative approach to leveraging AI and ML to enhance the application's functionality and user experience.

roles.title: Quality Assurance Engineer
responsibilities: Developing and executing comprehensive testing plans
Identifying and reporting defects and suggesting improvements
roles.vibe: Dedication to ensuring the application meets the highest standards of quality, reliability, and performance.

team.workflowRule: Agile development methodology with bi-weekly sprints, daily stand-ups, and continuous integration/continuous deployment (CI/CD) pipeline, ensuring rapid iteration and feedback incorporation.
team.synergyNotes: Roles collaborate closely through regular meetings and asynchronous communication, with the Lead Frontend Developer and Backend Developer working together on API integrations, the AI/ML Engineer contributing to feature development, and the Quality Assurance Engineer providing feedback across all stages of development.

workflow.description: Collecting and documenting the functional and non-functional requirements of the Todo App from the target audience and stakeholders.
workflow.input: User surveys, focus groups, and market research reports.
workflow.process: Conducting interviews, creating user personas, and defining the application's scope and objectives.
workflow.output: A comprehensive requirements document outlining the application's features, user interface, and technical specifications.

workflow.description: Creating the visual design and user experience of the Todo App based on the gathered requirements.
workflow.input: Requirements document, user personas, and design principles.
workflow.process: Sketching wireframes, designing high-fidelity prototypes, and testing with a small user group.
workflow.output: A functional prototype of the application's key features and a style guide for future development.

workflow.description: Building the server-side logic, database integration, and API connectivity of the Todo App.
workflow.input: Design prototype, technical specifications, and backend requirements.
workflow.process: Writing server-side code, integrating with databases, and developing API endpoints.
workflow.output: A fully functional backend infrastructure with API documentation.

workflow.description: Developing the client-side logic, integrating with the backend API, and conducting thorough testing of the Todo App.
workflow.input: Backend API, design prototype, and testing plans.
workflow.process: Writing client-side code, integrating with the backend API, and executing testing plans.
workflow.output: A fully functional and tested Todo App ready for deployment.

deliverables.name: Requirements Document
deliverables.description: A detailed document outlining the functional and non-functional requirements of the Todo App, including user personas, use cases, and technical specifications.

deliverables.name: Design Prototype
deliverables.description: A functional prototype of the Todo App's key features, including the user interface, user experience, and interaction design.

deliverables.name: Backend API
deliverables.description: A fully functional backend infrastructure with API endpoints for data retrieval and manipulation, including user authentication and authorization.

deliverables.name: Frontend Code
deliverables.format: Client-side code and integrated frontend-backend API
deliverables.description: The client-side logic of the Todo App, integrated with the backend API, including all features and functionalities.

deliverables.name: Testing Report
deliverables.description: A comprehensive report detailing the testing process, results, and any identified defects or areas for improvement.

deliverables.name: Deployment Plan
deliverables.description: A detailed plan outlining the steps and procedures for deploying the Todo App to a production environment, including setup, configuration, and monitoring.

qualityGates.remediationPath: If the requirements document is incomplete or the application scope is not clearly defined, revisit the requirements gathering phase and refine the document and scope definition.

criteria: User interface and user experience meet design principles
qualityGates.remediationPath: If the prototype is not functional or the UI/UX does not meet design principles, refine the prototype and redesign the UI/UX as necessary.

criteria: API endpoints are secure and well-documented
qualityGates.remediationPath: If the backend infrastructure is not fully functional or the API endpoints are not secure and well-documented, refactor the backend code and re-document the API endpoints.

criteria: Fully functional and tested frontend code
Application meets all requirements and functions as expected
qualityGates.remediationPath: If the frontend code is not fully functional or the application does not meet all requirements, refactor the frontend code and retest the application.

aiHandoffs.promptTemplate: Given a user's task history and the current date and time, generate a list of 5 todo list item suggestions that are relevant and actionable.
aiHandoffs.expectedOutputFormat: JSON array of todo list item suggestions
aiHandoffs.instructions: Refine the output by filtering out any suggestions that are not relevant to the user's current context or goals.

contextRequired: Time of day and day of the week
aiHandoffs.promptTemplate: Analyze a user's task completion history and identify patterns in their productivity, including the most productive times of day and days of the week.
aiHandoffs.instructions: Use the insights to provide personalized recommendations for improving the user's productivity and time management.

contextRequired: User's preferences for reminder timing and frequency
aiHandoffs.promptTemplate: Generate a set of personalized task reminders for a user based on their task list and preferences for reminder timing and frequency.
aiHandoffs.instructions: Refine the reminders by using a conversational tone and including relevant details about each task, such as deadlines and priorities.

risks.description: The risk of technical debt due to rushed development or inadequate testing, leading to bugs, performance issues, or security vulnerabilities.
risks.mitigation: Prioritize thorough testing, code reviews, and continuous integration/continuous deployment (CI/CD) to minimize technical debt and ensure the application's stability and security.

risks.description: The risk of not meeting user expectations or needs, resulting in low adoption rates or negative feedback.
risks.mitigation: Conduct user research, gather feedback, and iterate on the application's design and features to ensure it meets user needs and expectations.

risks.description: The risk of delays in development or deployment, leading to missed deadlines or lost opportunities.
risks.mitigation: Create a realistic project timeline, prioritize tasks, and allocate resources effectively to minimize delays and ensure timely completion.

risks.description: The risk of low user adoption or retention due to lack of awareness, poor user experience, or insufficient support.
risks.mitigation: Develop a comprehensive marketing strategy, provide excellent user support, and continuously gather feedback to improve the application and enhance user engagement.

name: TravelBlog
description: A simple, user-friendly website where travel enthusiasts can write, publish, and share their travel experiences through text articles, utilizing a straightforward frontend template for post display and a basic backend database for storage.
targetAudience: Casual readers and travel enthusiasts seeking to explore new destinations, cultures, and experiences through personal stories and reflections.

strategy.vision: To become the go-to online platform for travel storytelling, fostering a community of writers and readers who share a passion for exploration and cultural exchange, within the next five years.
strategy.mission: To design and develop an intuitive, accessible, and engaging website that simplifies the process of creating and sharing travel blogs, while ensuring a seamless user experience for both writers and readers.
strategy.uniqueValueProp: TravelBlog offers a unique blend of simplicity, community, and storytelling, allowing users to easily create and share their travel experiences, connect with like-minded individuals, and discover new destinations and cultures.

strategicPillars.title: User-Centric Design
strategicPillars.explanation: Prioritizing user experience and feedback to ensure the platform is intuitive, accessible, and engaging for both writers and readers, with a focus on simplicity and ease of use.

strategicPillars.title: Community Building
strategicPillars.explanation: Fostering a sense of community among users through features such as commenting, sharing, and user profiles, to encourage engagement, interaction, and a sense of belonging.

strategicPillars.title: Content Quality and Discovery
strategicPillars.explanation: Implementing algorithms and moderation processes to ensure high-quality content, while also providing features for users to discover new and relevant travel stories, destinations, and authors.

strategicPillars.title: Scalability and Performance
strategicPillars.explanation: Designing and developing the platform with scalability and performance in mind, to ensure seamless user experience even as the user base and content library grow.

positioningMatrix.notes: TravelBlog wins against incumbent solutions by offering a unique balance of simplicity, community features, and customization options, while also providing a seamless and engaging user experience.

roles.title: Lead Frontend Developer
responsibilities: Designing and developing the frontend template and user interface
roles.vibe: Focus on creating a seamless and engaging user experience, with attention to detail and a passion for frontend development.

roles.title: Backend Developer
responsibilities: Designing and developing the backend database and API
roles.vibe: Focus on creating a robust and scalable backend infrastructure, with attention to security and performance.

roles.title: Content Moderator
responsibilities: Ensuring content quality and adherence to community guidelines
roles.vibe: Focus on maintaining a high level of content quality and community engagement, with attention to detail and a passion for storytelling.

roles.title: DevOps Engineer
roles.vibe: Focus on creating a scalable and performant infrastructure, with attention to detail and a passion for DevOps.

team.workflowRule: RFC-First engineering with daily async check-ins and Slack-integrated status updates
team.synergyNotes: Roles pass work back and forth through a combination of code reviews, design critiques, and content moderation, ensuring maximum sprint velocity and a cohesive platform.

workflow.description: Conducting market research, defining the target audience, and creating a detailed project plan and timeline.
workflow.input: Market research reports, target audience personas, project plan and timeline
workflow.process: Conducting market research, defining the target audience, creating a project plan and timeline, and establishing key performance indicators (KPIs)
workflow.output: Project plan and timeline, target audience personas, KPIs

workflow.description: Designing and developing the frontend template and user interface.
workflow.input: Project plan and timeline, target audience personas, KPIs
workflow.process: Designing and developing the frontend template and user interface, conducting code reviews and testing
workflow.output: Frontend template and user interface, tested and reviewed

workflow.description: Designing and developing the backend database and API.
workflow.input: Project plan and timeline, target audience personas, KPIs
workflow.process: Designing and developing the backend database and API, conducting code reviews and testing
workflow.output: Backend database and API, tested and reviewed

workflow.description: Conducting thorough testing, deploying the platform, and ensuring scalability and performance.
workflow.input: Frontend template and user interface, backend database and API
workflow.process: Conducting thorough testing, deploying the platform, and ensuring scalability and performance

deliverables.name: Frontend Template
deliverables.description: A fully functional and tested frontend template, including user interface and user experience design.

deliverables.name: Backend Database and API
deliverables.description: A fully functional and tested backend database and API, including data security and scalability measures.
steps: Designing the backend database and API
Developing the backend database and API

deliverables.name: Content Moderation Guidelines
deliverables.description: A comprehensive set of guidelines for content moderation, including community guidelines and content review processes.

deliverables.name: Deployment Plan
deliverables.description: A detailed plan for deploying the platform, including infrastructure setup and scalability measures.

criteria: Project plan and timeline are complete and realistic
Target audience personas are well-defined and accurate
qualityGates.remediationPath: Revise project plan and timeline, redefine target audience personas

criteria: Frontend template and user interface are fully functional and tested
Code reviews and testing have been conducted
qualityGates.remediationPath: Revise frontend template and user interface, conduct additional testing

criteria: Backend database and API are fully functional and tested
Code reviews and testing have been conducted
qualityGates.remediationPath: Revise backend database and API, conduct additional testing

aiHandoffs.promptTemplate: Generate a high-quality, engaging article on [content topic] for [target audience personas], using a tone and style consistent with [tone and style guidelines].
aiHandoffs.instructions: Refine the generated content to ensure accuracy, clarity, and consistency with the platform's tone and style.

aiHandoffs.promptTemplate: Generate a high-quality, relevant image for [image topic], using a style consistent with [image style guidelines].
aiHandoffs.instructions: Refine the generated image to ensure accuracy, clarity, and consistency with the platform's style guidelines.

risks.description: The platform may not meet the needs and expectations of the target audience, resulting in low user engagement and retention.
risks.mitigation: Conduct thorough market research and user testing to ensure the platform meets the needs and expectations of the target audience, and make adjustments as necessary.

risks.description: The platform may experience technical issues, such as downtime or slow loading times, resulting in a poor user experience.
risks.mitigation: Implement robust testing and quality assurance processes, and ensure that the platform is designed and developed with scalability and performance in mind.

risks.description: The platform may not be launched on time, resulting in delays and lost opportunities.
risks.mitigation: Create a realistic project plan and timeline, and ensure that all team members are aware of their responsibilities and deadlines.
